articles.title: Energy Secretary Helps People with Bills
articles.summary: Ed Miliband, the Energy Secretary, says the government will help people if energy bills go up because of a war in Iran. He talked on a BBC show and promised to stand by people during this crisis. Oil and gas prices have risen, making petrol more expensive. The government is planning to give support to families who struggle with heating costs. Miliband warned petrol companies not to charge too much, and some companies got upset. He said there is evidence that petrol stations are not competing well on prices. The government will make sure companies do not take advantage of people during this difficult time.

articles.title: Prince William Shares a Special Photo for Mother's Day
articles.summary: Prince William shared a new photo of himself and his mother, Princess Diana, for Mother's Day. The picture was taken in 1984 when William was two years old, and they are sitting in a field of wildflowers at their home called Highgrove. William posted this on Instagram to remember his mother, who died in 1997. He also thought about other people who miss their moms on this day. The Royal Family shared more photos for Mother's Day, including ones of the late Queen and other family members. This shows how families remember and honor their mothers.

articles.title: Kimi Antonelli Wins His First F1 Race in China
articles.summary: Kimi Antonelli, a 19-year-old Italian driver, won his first Formula 1 race at the Chinese Grand Prix. He drove for Mercedes and finished first, with his teammate George Russell coming in second. This makes Antonelli the second youngest winner in F1 history, after Max Verstappen. He was very emotional after the race. The race had exciting battles, especially between Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc in their Ferraris. Hamilton got third place, his first podium finish with Ferrari. Some cars, like the McLarens, had to retire early. The crowd in China enjoyed the action.

articles.title: A New Friend Who Caused Big Money Problems
articles.summary: Ekaterina Barrett seemed like a rich and fancy lady who lived in expensive places like Mayfair and Monaco. She wore designer clothes and had Italian greyhounds. Bridget Hutchcroft owned a shop in London and met Barrett when she needed help with her dogs. Barrett asked Hutchcroft to watch her dogs, and they became friends. But later, Barrett borrowed a lot of money from Hutchcroft and never paid it back. Now, Hutchcroft says Barrett owes her over £1.6 million and is trying to get the money back through lawyers in different countries. This story shows how a friendship turned into a big financial problem for Hutchcroft.

articles.title: Iran's Women Soccer Players Return Home from Australia
articles.summary: Three members of Iran's women's soccer team have decided to go back to Iran instead of staying in Australia as refugees. They were part of a group of seven players and staff who had chosen to stay in Australia after a soccer tournament. Now, only three of them remain in Australia. The Australian government said the players were given chances to talk about their options before leaving. The team came to Australia for a soccer competition, and some players did not sing Iran's national anthem, which made people worry about their safety in Iran. Iranian groups and the U.S. president asked Australia to help the women. An Iranian news agency said the players' return was a failure for America and Australia.

articles.title: Big Winds Cause Big Problems in the U.S.
articles.summary: Strong winds hit parts of the United States, causing a lot of trouble. In states like Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Michigan, the winds knocked down trees and damaged homes, cars, and buildings. Many people lost power, with nearly 450,000 customers affected. In Nebraska, the winds made wildfires spread quickly, burning a huge area of land. One person died in the fires. Firefighters are working hard to put out the fires, but the strong winds make it difficult. People are helping by bringing water and food to support the firefighters. The winds were very fast, with speeds up to 85 mph in some places. This extreme weather shows how powerful nature can be and how it can disrupt our lives.
